title: Authors and Citation
text: Adrián Cidre González. Author, maintainer. Egor Kotov. Author. Rafael H. M. Pereira. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Cidre González , Kotov E, Pereira R (2026). duckh3: H3 extension 'DuckDB'. R package version 0.1.0, https://cidree.github.io/duckh3/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {duckh3: H3 extension of 'DuckDB'},   author = {Adrián {Cidre González} and Egor Kotov and Rafael H. M. Pereira},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://cidree.github.io/duckh3/}, }

title: H3 extension of DuckDB
text: {duckh3} provides fast, memory-efficient functions analysing manipulating large spatial/non-spatial datasets using H3 hierarchical indexing system R. bridges DuckDB’s H3 extension R’s data spatial ecosystems — particular {dplyr} {sf} — can leverage DuckDB’s analytical power without leaving familiar R workflow.

title: H3 extension of DuckDB
text: {duckh3} operates regular R data frames, tibbles, dbplyr lazy tables, duckspatial_df objects. Unlike purely spatial workflows, H3 operations require data spatial — table longitude/latitude columns, existing H3 index column, valid starting point. DuckDB connection used, H3 operations run inside connection H3 extension enabled, letting DuckDB apply query optimisations data reaches R. Results returned lazily materialised explicitly collect .

title: H3 extension of DuckDB
text: functions follow ddbh3_*() prefix (DuckDB H3). Function names structured around conversion perform, making easy discover right function: ddbh3_lonlat_to_*() — longitude/latitude coordinates H3 representations ddbh3_bigint_to_*() — H3 UBIGINT indexes representations ddbh3_strings_to_*() — H3 string indexes representations

title: H3 extension of DuckDB
text: Install stable release CRAN (available yet): Install latest GitHub version (features, fewer accumulated bugs): Install development version (may unstable):

title: H3 extension of DuckDB
text: central design principle {duckh3} H3 operation can used different ways depending data stored output format need. functions support three complementary output formats: Inputs can plain R data frames, lazy dbplyr tables, sf objects, duckspatial_df objects, making {duckh3} easy integrate non-spatial spatial pipelines. See Get Started vignette worked examples workflow.

title: H3 extension of DuckDB
text: Bug reports, feature requests, pull requests welcome! Raise issue Open pull request

what: Convert H3 bigint indexes to other representations — ddbh3_bigint_to
title: Convert H3 bigint indexes to other representations — ddbh3_bigint_to
text: Convert H3 cell indexes stored unsigned 64-bit integers (UBIGINT) formats (e.g. H3 strings)

title: Convert H3 bigint indexes to other representations — ddbh3_bigint_to
code: ddbh3_bigint_to_strings(   x,   h3bigint = "h3bigint",   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   new_column = "h3string",   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )  ddbh3_bigint_to_lon(   x,   h3bigint = "h3bigint",   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   new_column = "lon",   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )  ddbh3_bigint_to_lat(   x,   h3bigint = "h3bigint",   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   new_column = "lat",   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )  ddbh3_bigint_to_spatial(   x,   h3bigint = "h3bigint",   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Convert H3 bigint indexes to other representations — ddbh3_bigint_to
text: x Input spatial data. Can : duckspatial_df object (lazy spatial data frame via dbplyr) sf object tbl_lazy dbplyr character string naming table/view conn Data returned object. h3bigint name column x containing H3 UBIGINT conn connection object DuckDB database. NULL, function runs temporary DuckDB database. name character string length one specifying name table, character string length two specifying schema table names. NULL (default), function returns result sf object new_column Name new column create input data. NULL, function return vector result overwrite Boolean. whether overwrite existing table exists. Defaults FALSE. argument ignored name NULL. quiet logical value. TRUE, suppresses informational messages. Defaults FALSE.

title: Convert H3 bigint indexes to other representations — ddbh3_bigint_to
text: tbl_lazy x spatial, duckspatial_df x spatial (e.g. sf duckspatial_df). Alternatively, creates table connection name provided, returns TRUE invisibly.

title: Convert H3 bigint indexes to other representations — ddbh3_bigint_to
text: four functions differ output format: ddbh3_bigint_to_strings() converts H3 indexes strings (e.g. "8928308280fffff") ddbh3_bigint_to_spatial() converts H3 indexes spatial hexagon polygons ddbh3_bigint_to_lon() extracts longitude H3 cell centroid ddbh3_bigint_to_lat() extracts latitude H3 cell centroid

title: Convert H3 bigint indexes to other representations — ddbh3_bigint_to

what: Create a DuckDB connection with spatial and h3 extensions — ddbh3_create_conn
title: Create a DuckDB connection with spatial and h3 extensions — ddbh3_create_conn
text: creates DuckDB connection, installs loads spatial h3 extensions

title: Create a DuckDB connection with spatial and h3 extensions — ddbh3_create_conn
Create a DuckDB connection with spatial and h3 extensions — ddbh3_create_conn
text: dbdir String. Either "tempdir" "memory". Defaults "memory". threads Integer. Number threads use. NULL (default), setting changed, DuckDB engine use available cores detects (warning, shared HPC nodes detected number cores might total number cores node, per-job allocation). memory_limit_gb Numeric. Memory limit GB. NULL (default), setting changed, DuckDB engine use 80% available operating system memory detects (warning, shared HPC nodes detected memory might full node memory, per-job allocation).

title: Create a DuckDB connection with spatial and h3 extensions — ddbh3_create_conn
text: duckdb_connection

title: Create a DuckDB connection with spatial and h3 extensions — ddbh3_create_conn
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # load packages library(duckspatial) library(duckh3)  # create a duckdb database in memory conn <- ddbh3_create_conn(dbdir = "memory")  # create a duckdb database in disk conn <- ddbh3_create_conn(dbdir = "tempdir")  # create a connection with 1 thread and 2GB memory limit conn <- ddbh3_create_conn(threads = 1, memory_limit_gb = 2) ddbs_stop_conn(conn) } # }

what: Convert longitude and latitude to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_lonlat_to
title: Convert longitude and latitude to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_lonlat_to
text: Convert geographic coordinates (longitude latitude) H3 cell representations specified resolution, different output formats

title: Convert longitude and latitude to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_lonlat_to
code: ddbh3_lonlat_to_strings(   x,   lon = "lon",   lat = "lat",   resolution = 8,   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   new_column = "h3string",   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )  ddbh3_lonlat_to_spatial(   x,   lon = "lon",   lat = "lat",   resolution = 8,   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )  ddbh3_lonlat_to_bigint(   x,   lon = "lon",   lat = "lat",   resolution = 8,   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   new_column = "h3bigint",   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Convert longitude and latitude to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_lonlat_to
text: x Input spatial data. Can : duckspatial_df object (lazy spatial data frame via dbplyr) sf object tbl_lazy dbplyr character string naming table/view conn Data returned object. lon name column x containing longitude lat name column x containing latitude resolution number specifying resolution level H3 string (0 15) conn connection object DuckDB database. NULL, function runs temporary DuckDB database. name character string length one specifying name table, character string length two specifying schema table names. NULL (default), function returns result sf object new_column Name new column create input data. NULL, function return vector result overwrite Boolean. whether overwrite existing table exists. Defaults FALSE. argument ignored name NULL. quiet logical value. TRUE, suppresses informational messages. Defaults FALSE.

title: Convert longitude and latitude to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_lonlat_to
text: tbl_lazy x spatial, duckspatial_df x spatial (e.g. sf duckspatial_df). Alternatively, creates table connection name provided, returns TRUE invisibly.

title: Convert longitude and latitude to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_lonlat_to
text: three functions differ output format H3 cell index: ddbh3_lonlat_to_strings() returns H3 cell indexes strings (e.g. "8928308280fffff") ddbh3_lonlat_to_bigint() returns H3 cell indexes unsigned 64-bit integers (UBIGINT) ddbh3_lonlat_to_spatial() returns H3 cells spatial hexagon polygons

title: Convert longitude and latitude to H3 cell representations — ddbh3_lonlat_to

what: Convert H3 string indexes to other representations — ddbh3_strings_to
title: Convert H3 string indexes to other representations — ddbh3_strings_to
text: Convert H3 cell indexes stored strings representations: ddbh3_strings_to_bigint(), ddbh3_strings_to_spatial(), ddbh3_strings_to_lon(), ddbh3_strings_to_lat().

title: Convert H3 string indexes to other representations — ddbh3_strings_to
code: ddbh3_strings_to_bigint(   x,   h3string = "h3string",   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   new_column = "h3bigint",   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )  ddbh3_strings_to_lon(   x,   h3string = "h3string",   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   new_column = "lon",   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )  ddbh3_strings_to_lat(   x,   h3string = "h3string",   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   new_column = "lat",   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )  ddbh3_strings_to_spatial(   x,   h3string = "h3string",   conn = NULL,   name = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Convert H3 string indexes to other representations — ddbh3_strings_to
text: x Input spatial data. Can : duckspatial_df object (lazy spatial data frame via dbplyr) sf object tbl_lazy dbplyr character string naming table/view conn Data returned object. h3string name column x containing H3 strings conn connection object DuckDB database. NULL, function runs temporary DuckDB database. name character string length one specifying name table, character string length two specifying schema table names. NULL (default), function returns result sf object new_column Name new column create input data. NULL, function return vector result overwrite Boolean. whether overwrite existing table exists. Defaults FALSE. argument ignored name NULL. quiet logical value. TRUE, suppresses informational messages. Defaults FALSE.

title: Convert H3 string indexes to other representations — ddbh3_strings_to
text: tbl_lazy x spatial, duckspatial_df x spatial (e.g. sf duckspatial_df). Alternatively, creates table connection name provided, returns TRUE invisibly.

text: four functions differ output format: ddbh3_strings_to_bigint() converts H3 indexes unsigned 64-bit integers (UBIGINT) ddbh3_strings_to_spatial() converts H3 indexes spatial hexagon polygons ddbh3_strings_to_lon() extracts longitude H3 cell centroid ddbh3_strings_to_lat() extracts latitude H3 cell centroid
